flags.message: The snapshot is a bare docket shell: no docket entries, no docket number, no filing/argument/termination dates, no panel, no cert dates. The only substantive signal is a linked published opinion cluster. The caption style ('Lambert's Lessee v. Payne', a fictional-lessee ejectment caption) marks this as an early-1800s case that reached the Court by writ of error as of right, so a 'petition disposition' (P(granted)) framing does not map onto it - there was no discretionary petition to grant or deny. The prediction is therefore base-rate-anchored (disposition 'other', P(granted)=0.02) rather than fact-driven. Consider excluding pre-certiorari-era legacy dockets from petition-disposition events or giving them an era-appropriate event kind.
